text: Soy sauce-aroma type baijiu (SSAB), a unique subcategory of Chinese baijiu, is generally believed to be the product of quality base liquor. Therefore, understanding the relationship between the quality grade and the composition and contents of the key components in the base liquor is of great significance for not only the flavor characteristics of SSAB but also for the safe consumption of SSAB by consumers. In this study, the quality grades and composition and contents of key components in SSAB base liquor were analyzed by means of combination of sensory evaluation, E-nose, and headspace solid-phase microextraction microextraction gas chromatography-mass spectrometry (HS-SPME-GC-MS). The results showed that higher quality grade liquor was associated with more sauce-like, floral, fruity and fermented aroma and characteristics of soft texture, complete mouthfeel and long-lasting flavor. The E-nose was also able to distinguish between different quality grades of liquors. By means of untargeted metabolomics using HS-SPME-GC-MS, it was found that 16 differential components were detected throughout the fermentation process for all different samples, which consisted of 11 types of esters, 1 type of alcohol, 2 types of aldehydes and 2 types of ketones. Further quantitative analysis showed that the better the quality of liquors, the more abundant the aromatic compounds, alcohols and ketones contained, and the analysis by principal component analysis (PCA) showed that higher quality liquors possessed a stronger fruity flavor. In addition, correlation analysis showed that furfuryl ethyl ether, ethyl butyrate, isopentyl hexanoate, ethyl nonanoate and 3-methyl-1-butanol had the most significant effects on the sensor responses of E-nose, thus digitizing the sensory differences in the quality grades of liquors and providing a new approach for quality control and standardization of SSAB base liquors.
